generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalAirTemperatureSensorHotSpot : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision DS18S20 1-Wire digital thermometer measuring internal air temperature at the hottest position near the heatbed. Accuracy: ±0.5°C (range: -55°C to +125°C). Connected via 1-Wire bus to Raspberry Pi GPIO."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalAirTemperatureDataHotSpot
]

instance InternalAirTemperatureSensorColdSpot : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision DS18S20 1-Wire digital thermometer measuring internal air temperature at the coldest position. Accuracy: ±0.5°C (range: -55°C to +125°C). Connected via 1-Wire bus to Raspberry Pi GPIO."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalAirTemperatureDataColdSpot
]

instance ExternalRoomTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision DS18S20 1-Wire digital thermometer measuring external room temperature. Accuracy: ±0.5°C (range: -55°C to +125°C). Connected via 1-Wire bus to Raspberry Pi GPIO."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalRoomTemperatureData
]

instance HeatbedPowerMonitor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Indirect sensing component monitoring electrical power input (V×I×Δt) to the heatbed for energy balance calculations in thermal models."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HeatbedPowerInputData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalAirTemperatureDataHotSpot : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalAirTemperatureSensorHotSpot
]

instance InternalAirTemperatureDataColdSpot : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalAirTemperatureSensorColdSpot
]

instance ExternalRoomTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalRoomTemperatureSensor
]

instance HeatbedPowerInputData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HeatbedPowerMonitor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ThermalModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified thermal model using equation: dT_bair/dt = [V*I*Δt - G_box*(T_bair - T_room)] / C_air. Free parameters: C_air (thermal capacity of air), G_box (heat transfer coefficient to box walls). Assumes all electrical power converts to heat and heatbed has no thermal inertia."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance ThermalModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Advanced thermal model with two equations: dT_heater/dt = [V*I*Δt - G_heater*(T_heater - T_bair)] / C_heater and dT_bair/dt = [G_heater*(T_heater - T_bair) - G_box*(T_bair - T_room)] / C_air. Free parameters: C_heater, C_air, G_heater, G_box. Captures thermal inertia of heatbed for higher fidelity."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance InternalAirTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Averaged time-series temperature data from internal air sensors (hot and cold spots) representing uniform temperature distribution assumption. Used for model validation and control decisions."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalAirTemperatureDataHotSpot, InternalAirTemperatureDataColdSpot
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler, ControlOptimizationEnabler
]

instance ExternalRoomTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series room temperature data used as reference for heat loss calculations (G_box parameter) in thermal models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalRoomTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance HeatbedPowerInputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series electrical power input data (V×I×Δt) to heatbed used for energy balance calculations in thermal models and control decisions."
    DTDFVocab:fromData HeatbedPowerInputData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler, ControlOptimizationEnabler
]

instance FanVoltageData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series fan voltage levels (12V/18V) used to analyze temperature uniformity and adjust control parameters."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlOptimizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based calibration tool using Scipy's least_squares for non-linear parameter optimization. Minimizes residual error between predicted and measured temperatures for both Model A and Model B."
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperaturePredictionService, ModelComparisonService, ParameterOptimizationService
]

instance ControlOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Bang-bang-like controller implementation with configurable parameters (LL, UL, H, C). Executes temperature regulation logic based on sensor feedback and model predictions."
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureControlService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source message broker enabling asynchronous communication between Physical Twin components and Digital Twin services. Handles data transmission from sensors and control commands to actuators."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataProcessingService, ControlCommandService
]

instance LowLevelDriver : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Hardware abstraction layer for sensor polling and actuator control. Manages 1-Wire bus communication with DS18S20 sensors and MOSFET/optocoupler interfaces for heatbed/fan control."
    DTDFVocab:enables SensorDataAcquisitionService, ActuatorControlService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data visualization tool generating time-series plots showing temperature uniformity, heatbed power input, and fan status for operational monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables OperationalMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance TemperaturePredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time thermal behavior prediction using either Model A (simplified) or Model B (high-fidelity). Model B preferred for capturing heatbed thermal inertia."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureUniformityInsight, ModelPerformanceInsight
]

instance TemperatureControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Bang-bang-like temperature regulation with tunable parameters (LL, UL, H, C). Adjusts heatbed and fan operations to maintain ±0.5°C accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlTuningAction, FanVoltageAdjustmentAction
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection by comparing predicted (Model B) and measured temperatures. Identifies sensor failures or heatbed inefficiencies."
    DTDFVocab:provides SensorFailureDetectionAction, HeatbedEfficiencyInsight
]

instance ModelComparisonService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Compares Model A and Model B predictions to determine which better fits the Physical Twin's thermal behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelFidelityInsight
]

instance ParameterOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Recommends updates to thermal parameters (C_air, G_box, C_heater, G_heater) based on calibration results."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterUpdateAction
]

instance OperationalMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates visualizations (time-series plots) of temperature uniformity, heatbed power, and fan status for manual decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureUniformityVisualizationInsight
]

instance ExperimentalDesignService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Guides further experiments by suggesting test conditions (e.g., higher fan voltages) based on current performance metrics."
    DTDFVocab:provides ExperimentalConditionRecommendationAction
]

instance RealTimeDataProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Processes time-series sensor data and actuator states in real-time for immediate model updates and control decisions."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataProcessingInsight
]

instance ControlCommandService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Transmits control commands from Digital Twin to Physical Twin actuators via RabbitMQ message broker."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuatorCommandAction
]

instance SensorDataAcquisitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Collects and formats time-series data from DS18S20 sensors via 1-Wire bus for processing by Digital Twin components."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataProcessingInsight
]

instance ActuatorControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes control commands to heatbed and fan actuators through MOSFET/optocoupler interfaces based on Digital Twin decisions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuatorCommandAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureUniformityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identifies hot/cold spots with ≤7°C discrepancy (reduced to ≤6°C with 18V fan). Suggests fan voltage adjustments for improved uniformity."
]

instance ModelPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Compares prediction accuracy between Model A and Model B to determine optimal model for current operating conditions."
]

instance ModelFidelityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Evaluates which thermal model (A or B) better represents the Physical Twin's actual thermal behavior based on residual error analysis."
]

instance SensorFailureDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggers alerts when discrepancies between predicted and measured temperatures exceed calibration thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HeatbedEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assesses heatbed performance based on energy balance calculations and temperature response times."
]

instance ControlTuningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjusts controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) to optimize temperature regulation performance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FanVoltageAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommends or implements fan voltage changes (e.g., from 12V to 18V) to improve temperature uniformity."
]

instance ParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Updates thermal model parameters (C_air, G_box, C_heater, G_heater) based on calibration results."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ExperimentalConditionRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggests new experimental conditions (e.g., higher fan voltages) for testing temperature regulation improvements."
]

instance TemperatureUniformityVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides visual representations of temperature distribution within the incubator, highlighting uniformity metrics."
]

instance DataProcessingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Ensures real-time processing of sensor data and actuator states for immediate model updates and control decisions."
]

instance ActuatorCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Transmits control signals to heatbed and fan actuators via low-level driver and RabbitMQ message broker."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "214×214×3 mm surface heat source delivering up to 100W (100J/s) of power. Controlled via MOSFETs and optocouplers for signal isolation."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "12V/24V fan with 0.02 m³/s airflow for air circulation. Operates at variable voltages (tested at 12V and 18V) to ensure uniform temperature distribution."
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Central controller executing bang-bang-like control strategy with configurable parameters (LL, UL, H, C) to regulate heatbed and fan based on sensor feedback."
]

// C1: System Under Study
instance ThermalIncubatorPhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Cyber-Physical System designed to regulate and maintain target temperature inside an insulated styrofoam box (air volume: 0.03 m³, mass: 0.04 kg). Components include heatbed, fan, DS18S20 sensors, Raspberry Pi 4, PCB with MOSFETs/optocouplers, and power supply."
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized Digital Twin implementation on Raspberry Pi 4 comprising two thermal models (A and B), RabbitMQ server, low-level driver, and bang-bang controller. Supports real-time temperature prediction, control optimization, and fault detection."
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorEnvironment
]

instance ThermalIncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains ThermalIncubatorPhysicalTwin, DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **Virtual-to-Physical (V2P) interaction** consists of control commands generated by the DT and executed on the PT:
  1. **Actuator Commands**:
     - **Heatbed Control**: Binary ON/OFF signals (via PWM) sent through RabbitMQ to the low-level driver, which uses MOSFETs to switch the heatbed’s power supply. Duration is governed by the **H (Heating Duration)** parameter.
     - **Fan Control**: Voltage level commands (12V or 18V) to adjust airflow strength, transmitted via RabbitMQ to the low-level driver.
  2. **Controller Parameters**:
     - The DT provides the **LL (Lower Limit)**, **UL (Upper Limit)**, **H**, and **C** parameters to the controller, which are used to determine when to actuate the heatbed/fan.
  3. **Safety Protocols**:
     - The low-level driver enforces a **safe state protocol** to prevent unsafe conditions (e.g., overheating) by validating commands before execution. This is a **physical feature** of the driver, not a direct V2P command.
  **Note**: Model calibration (e.g., updating G_box, C_air, G_heater) is a **DT-internal process** and does not directly interact with the PT. Fault detection (e.g., temperature spikes) triggers alerts but does not modify the PT’s state."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin (DT)** operates on the following time scales: (1) **Heating Dynamics**: The heatbed can raise the air temperature from 20°C to 26.85°C in **<2 seconds** (200J energy requirement at ≥100W power). (2) **Air Circulation**: The fan (0.02 m³/s airflow) fully circulates the box’s 0.03 m³ air volume in **~2 seconds**. (3) **Control Loop**: The **bang-bang controller** executes in discrete steps with configurable **heating duration (H)** and **cooldown duration (C)**, implying a **sub-second to second-scale** response time. (4) **Data Polling**: Sensor data is periodically read by the **low-level driver** (frequency not specified but implied to be real-time for control). (5) **Model Calibration**: Offline optimization of model parameters uses **non-linear least squares**, with no explicit time constraints mentioned but assumed to be batch-processed."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin (DT) constellation** is organized as follows:
  1. **Architecture**:
     - **Centralized**: All DT components (models, controller, RabbitMQ) reside on the **Raspberry Pi**, co-located with the PT.
     - **Modular Layers**:
       - **Physical Layer (PT)**: Hardware components (heatbed, fan, sensors).
       - **Cyber Layer (DT)**:
         - **Sensing Layer**: Low-level driver collects sensor data.
         - **Communication Layer**: RabbitMQ handles message routing.
         - **Control Layer**: Bang-bang controller executes temperature regulation.
         - **Modeling Layer**: Thermal models (A/B) predict PT behavior.
  2. **Data Flow**:
     - **PT → DT**: Sensor data and actuator states are published to RabbitMQ queues.
     - **DT → PT**: Control commands are subscribed and executed by the low-level driver.
     - **Feedback Loop**: Model predictions are compared to real-time data for calibration.
  3. **Orchestration**:
     - **Event-Driven**: Triggers include temperature threshold crossings, power input changes, and calibration completion.
     - **Decoupled Components**: RabbitMQ enables loose coupling between PT and DT modules."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin (DT)** covers the following **life-cycle stages** of the Physical Twin (PT):
  1. **As Designed (Ideal)**:
     - **Model Development**: Theoretical models (Model A/B) are derived based on **assumptions** (e.g., uniform temperature, negligible heat transfer to walls).
     - **Simulation**: Offline simulations are run to validate model behavior before deployment.
  2. **As Manufactured**:
     - **Hardware Assembly**: The PT is built with specified components (styrofoam box, heatbed, PCB, Raspberry Pi).
     - **Initial Calibration**: Free parameters (**C_air**, **G_box**, etc.) are calibrated using **real-world data** from the PT.
  3. **As Operated**:
     - **Real-Time Monitoring**: The DT continuously synchronizes with the PT via **RabbitMQ**, updating models and control parameters based on operational data.
     - **Adaptive Reconfiguration**: The DT can adjust to changes in the PT (e.g., sensor drift, heatbed degradation) through **online calibration**.
  The DT does not explicitly cover **end-of-life** stages (e.g., decommissioning) but could be extended to include **predictive maintenance** for component failure forecasting."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **fidelity and validity** of the DT are addressed through:
  1. **Model Assumptions and Trade-offs**:
     - **Model A**: Simpler (2 parameters) but ignores heatbed thermal inertia (**Assumption 4**).
     - **Model B**: More complex (4 parameters) but captures heatbed dynamics, improving accuracy for **thermal inertia prediction**.
  2. **Validation Mechanisms**:
     - **Uniformity Testing**: Experiments (Fig. 10, Fig. 12) show temperature discrepancies of **≤7°C** (reduced to **≤6°C** with higher fan voltage), validating **Assumption 1**.
     - **Calibration Accuracy**: Non-linear least squares minimizes residual error between predicted and measured temperatures.
     - **Control Stability**: Bang-bang controller parameters (**H**, **C**) are tuned to prevent overshoot.
  3. **Uncertainty Sources**:
     - **Sensor Accuracy**: DS18S20 has ±0.5°C error, affecting model calibration.
     - **Environmental Factors**: Room temperature fluctuations (**T_room**) introduce variability in **G_box**.
     - **Model Simplifications**: Assumptions (e.g., constant specific heat capacity) may reduce fidelity in extreme conditions.
  4. **Verification Methods**:
     - **Experimental Comparison**: Real-time data (Fig. 10) is used to validate model predictions.
     - **Parameter Optimization**: Scipy’s `least_squares` ensures parameters minimize prediction error."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical connection** between the PT and DT is established via:
  1. **Communication Protocol**:
     - **RabbitMQ**: An open-source message broker using the **AMQP 0-9-1** protocol (inferred from RabbitMQ’s default). Supports queues/exchanges for routing messages between the controller, low-level driver, and DT.
     - **1-Wire Bus**: Used for sensor communication, with each DS18S20 sensor addressed via its unique 64-bit serial code.
  2. **Network Architecture**:
     - **Local Deployment**: RabbitMQ and the DT run on the same Raspberry Pi, minimizing latency.
     - **Cloud Potential**: RabbitMQ supports remote deployment, enabling distributed DT architectures (e.g., models hosted in the cloud while the PT remains local).
  3. **Data Encoding**:
     - No explicit mention of serialization formats (e.g., JSON, XML), but the open-source repository suggests structured payloads (e.g., temperature values, actuator commands).
  4. **Hardware Interfaces**:
     - **GPIO**: Raspberry Pi’s GPIO pins connect to the PCB, which interfaces with sensors (1-Wire) and actuators (MOSFETs for heatbed, direct fan control).
     - **Power Supply**: Heatbed and fan are powered via separate circuits, with the Raspberry Pi providing control signals."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin (DT)** is hosted and deployed as follows:
  1. **Hardware Hosting**:
     - **Primary Host**: **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B**, which also hosts the PT controller.
     - **Co-Location**: DT components (RabbitMQ, models, controller) run on the same device as the PT to minimize latency.
  2. **Software Deployment**:
     - **RabbitMQ Server**: Deployed on the Raspberry Pi for local message brokering.
     - **Low-Level Driver**: Runs as a background service to interface with sensors/actuators.
     - **Controller**: Implements the bang-bang logic with configurable parameters (**LL**, **UL**, **H**, **C**).
     - **Model Calibration**: Executed via Python scripts using Scipy’s optimization tools.
  3. **Scalability**:
     - **Single-Node**: Current deployment is monolithic but designed for modularity (e.g., RabbitMQ can be extended to a cluster).
     - **Open-Source**: All code is available on [GitHub](https://github.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator), enabling custom deployment (e.g., cloud-based DT with remote PT).
  4. **Technology Stack**:
     - **Operating System**: Raspberry Pi OS (Linux-based).
     - **Programming Languages**: Python for modeling/calibration, C/C++ for low-level driver (if applicable).
     - **Dependencies**: RabbitMQ, Scipy, and standard GPIO libraries."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin (DT)** adheres to the following **standards**: (1) **Units**: All models and calculations use **SI units** (e.g., Joules for energy, Kelvin for temperature). (2) **Communication Protocol**: **RabbitMQ** uses the **AMQP (Advanced Message Queuing Protocol)** for message brokering (version not specified). (3) **Sensor Interface**: **1-Wire bus protocol** for multi-sensor connectivity. (4) **Open-Source Licensing**: The system is released under an **open-source license** (specific license not mentioned in the document). (5) **Hardware Standards**: Components like the **DS18S20 sensor** and **Raspberry Pi** follow industry-standard interfaces (e.g., GPIO, 1-Wire). No proprietary or domain-specific standards are referenced."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin (DT)** incorporates the following **safety considerations**: (1) **Hardware Isolation**: **MOSFETs and optocouplers** on the PCB decouple Raspberry Pi GPIO signals from high-current actuation circuits (e.g., heatbed), preventing damage from voltage spikes. (2) **Safe Protocol**: The **low-level driver** enforces safety checks before executing control commands, ensuring the PT remains in a safe state. (3) **Temperature Limits**: The **bang-bang controller** uses **LL (Lower Limit)** and **UL (Upper Limit)** parameters to prevent overheating or excessive cooling. (4) **Redundancy**: Three temperature sensors provide cross-validation of measurements. **Security considerations are not explicitly addressed** in the document, including aspects like authentication, encryption, or tamper-proofing of data or commands. The system assumes a trusted environment for deployment."
]
